advantages: Thoughtful imputation lets algorithms that require complete inputs train on more rows than aggressive deletion alone.
Median and group-wise imputation are simple, fast baselines that stabilize skewed numeric columns better than mean fill.
Missingness indicators preserve the signal that a value was absent—useful when MNAR is plausible.
Tree models with native NaN support can learn informative split directions without forcing a single substitute value.
disadvantages: Mean or median imputation shrinks variance and understates uncertainty, which can overstate model confidence.
Listwise deletion wastes rows and can bias estimates when missingness is not MCAR.
Model-based imputation (KNN, MICE) costs compute and can leak target information if fit improperly across folds.
Blind forward-fill on long gaps invents flat segments that never occurred in the physical process.
